title: Tesla erases the past, CAT electrifies the future, and EV tech training TODAY
summary:
On today’s time-traveling episode of Quick Charge, Tesla might be trying to erase evidence of FSD fraud, Caterpillar keeps trying to electrify the construction space, and time is rapidly running out on what’s left of the 30% home solar tax credit.

summary:
What’s another $200? Prices for the 2027 Cadillac Lyriq increase by just $200 for the 2027 model year, but it will likely be worth it.

title: You’ve got ONE MONTH to safe harbor your solar project and get 30% off
summary:
The window to claim a 30% tax credit on your solar panel project is closing July 4th, which means you’ve got just one month left to safe harbor your project and preserve your eligibility – keep reading to find out how.

title: Houston gets a solar, storage + electricity bundle for 6¢ per kWh
summary:
Houston homeowners are getting a new way to get solar and battery storage without signing up for a decades-long loan.

title: BYD converts its popular electric hatch into a van with ‘class-leading range’ for $40,000
summary:
BYD launched the purely electric van based on the Dolphin hatchback, one of the brand’s most popular EVs globally.

more…

title: Audi reveals 1,001-hp Nuvolari hybrid supercar — so much for going all-electric
summary:
Audi unveiled the Nuvolari today, a 1,001-hp hybrid supercar powered by a 4.0-liter V8 biturbo and three electric motors. Limited to 499 units at roughly $700,000 each, it is the most powerful and fastest production vehicle in the brand’s history.

The reveal is notable for what it represents beyond raw performance. Audi pledged in 2021 to launch only electric vehicles starting in 2026 — and instead, it just unveiled a V8-powered supercar as its flagship.

more…

title: White House will dump $700M of public funds into costly, unreliable coal again
summary:
The White House is announcing another waste of taxpayer dollars today, spending $700M to try to revive a dead energy source, saddling Americans with more pollution and infrastructure that is destined to go unused in short order.

more…

title: Jackery HomePower 3600 Plus power station exclusives from $1,399, EGO Select Cut Touch Drive mower $543 low, Segway, more
summary:
Leading our Thursday Green Deals is a pair of exclusive deals on Jackery’s 3,584Wh HomePower 3600 Plus Portable Power Station with or without a 500W solar panel at their second-lowest prices starting from $1,399. Right behind that, we spotted the advanced EGO Power+ 21-inch Select Cut Touch Drive Cordless Electric Lawn Mower with 2x 4.0Ah batteries and a rapid charger hitting a new $543 low. We also have two Segway deals, the first being on the new Navimow X430 Robot Lawn Mower coming with a FREE Garage X attachment at $2,499, while the brand’s 1,024Wh Cube 1000 and 2,048Wh Cube 2000 power stations are starting from $330 – plus, more deals waiting for you below. We also have all the hangover deals collected down at the bottom of the page, like yesterday’s Bluetti RV Season Sale lineup with two exclusive bonus savings codes for our readers, and more.

Head below for other New Green Deals we’ve found today and, of course, Electrek’s best EV buying and leasing deals. Also, check out the new Electrek Tesla Shop for the best deals on Tesla accessories.

title: Nissan joins new ‘Cheaper Than China’ solid-state EV battery project
summary:
After announcing a new three-year collaboration with Nissan to advance low-cost, solid-state EV batteries, Gelion’s tech may be the secret to leveling the playing field with China.

title: BMW’s new iX3 drove 485 miles on a single charge, the longest-range EV in NAF’s test
summary:
BMW’s new iX3 beat out the competition, driving 485 miles on a single charge, making it the longest-range EV during Motor and NAF’s summer range test.

title: Tesla Wants Its Cameras To Blink Like a Human Eye
summary: Computers might be better at some things, but Tesla's newest patent shows that the human eye remains undefeated in one important area.

title: This 85% Scale Vintage Bentley Is One Of A Kind—And Electric
summary: The bespoke Bentley Blower Junior was commissioned by American actor Jason Momoa.

title: The Polestar 2 Is Returning To North America
summary: The fan favorite EV is going back on sale in North America, thanks to Canada’s openness to Chinese-built electric cars.

title: The Volvo XC60 Plug-In Hybrid Will Get A Serious Range Boost: Report
summary: The current XC60 T8 plug-in hybrid can go 35 miles on electric power. The 2028 model will reportedly nearly triple that.

title: It's Still Way More Expensive To Insure An EV. But That's Changing
summary: Newer EVs cost about 18% more to insure than gas vehicles. Here's why.

title: The Rolls-Royce Spectre Gets Big Update. But You Wouldn’t Know It
summary: It now goes farther, charges faster, and responds more sharply, but the design remains mostly untouched.

title: The Company That Builds The iPhone Just Unveiled A Tesla Model Y Competitor
summary: It’s no Apple Car, but Foxconn’s Cavira gets an LFP battery and a dual-motor powertrain that can deliver up to 468 horsepower.

title: Tesla Is Selling Discounted Used Cybertrucks Now
summary: The cheapest used Cybertruck in Tesla’s inventory is less than $67,000. Is that enough to convince buyers, though?

title: Lexus Cancels Next-Generation EV Sedan
summary: Lexus was developing a production version of the dramatic LF-ZC electric sedan concept, an IS EV, but it’s abandoning the project.

title: Tesla Could Build A Model 3 Plaid. That Doesn’t Mean It Will
summary: Tesla’s top engineer says he thinks about a tri-motor Model 3 “all the time,” but the company’s priorities lie elsewhere.
